title: Foreign Credential Recognition Program Approved Funding - Canada.ca

## List of approved projects:
Organization United Brotherhood of Carpenters and Joiners of America Canadian District
Location of Organization Winnipeg, MB
ESDC Contribution $9,023,177
Start Date 2024-10-21
Project Description To increase Canada's skilled labour supply by helping internationally trained workers become Red Seal certified through tailored employment supports and specialized curriculum development.
Organization Windmill Microlending
Location of Organization Toronto, ON
ESDC Contribution $626,411
Start Date 2024-09-23
Project Description To help internationally educated healthcare professionals restart their careers in Canada and address healthcare labour shortages by providing necessary support, resources, and skills for successful integration into the sector.
Organization Health Association of African Canadians
Location of Organization Cherry Brook, NS
ESDC Contribution $1,329,187
Start Date 2024-09-10
Project Description To enhance the integration of internationally educated nurses (IENs) into the Canadian labour market by offering employment supports, focusing on IENs of African and Caribbean descent in Atlantic Canada and major cities like Toronto, Calgary, and Montreal.
Organization La Société Économique de l'Ontario (SÉO)
Location of Organization Ottawa, ON
ESDC Contribution $2,478,500
Start Date 2024-08-08
Project Description To improve the integration of skilled newcomer women who are internationally trained healthcare professionals into the Canadian labour market, focusing on various healthcare professions in Ontario and British Columbia.
Organization S.U.C.C.E.S.S.
Location of Organization Vancouver, BC
ESDC Contribution $9,900,000
Start Date 2024-07-01
Project Description To help internationally educated health professionals, including immigrants and refugees, access information, resources, and services to integrate into the Canadian healthcare sector.
Organization Government of Newfoundland and Labrador
Location of Organization St. John's, NL
ESDC Contribution $5,141,635
Start Date 2024-03-27
Project Description To support internationally educated health professionals by providing pre-arrival training and employment support to facilitate their integration into the Canadian labour market in Newfoundland and Labrador.
Organization Alberta Health Services
Location of Organization Edmonton, AB
ESDC Contribution $9,985,656
Start Date 2024-03-27
Project Description To support internationally trained health professionals by providing employment assistance and system improvements to ensure their integration into healthcare positions that match their career goals and skills.
Organization Association of Canadian Faculties of Dentistry
Location of Organization Winnipeg, MB
ESDC Contribution $8,306,400
Start Date 2024-01-18
Project Description To create and assess a new certification pathway for internationally trained dentists, streamline FCR processes and expedite their entry into the labour market across four Canadian provinces.
Organization Alberta International Medical Graduates Association (AIMGA)
Location of Organization Calgary, AB
ESDC Contribution $1,464,400
Start Date 2024-01-15
Project Description To enhance a web-based tool for career advisors by expanding its scope to include job platform integration, connect jobseekers with mentors, improved accuracy and user interface, and additional career options.
Organization BC College of Nurses and Midwives (Inspire Global Assessments)
Location of Organization Vancouver, BC
ESDC Contribution $9,934,155
Start Date 2024-01-15
Project Description To virtualize the Nursing Community Assessment Service to reduce costs, expand reach, and expedite the entry of internationally educated nurses into safe nursing roles by streamlining the credentialing process and enhancing access to skills assessments.
Organization Government of Yukon
Location of Organization Whitehorse, YK
ESDC Contribution $3,650,286
Start Date 2024-01-15
Project Description To create a territorial system that collaborates with provincial regulators to recognize foreign credentials, facilitating the integration of internationally educated healthcare professionals and providing Canadian work experience.
Organization Canadian Pharmacists Association
Location of Organization Ottawa, ON
ESDC Contribution $5,186,097
Start Date 2024-01-08
Project Description To offer paid work experience, mentoring, and training through placements in partnering pharmacies across Canada, enhancing the knowledge and understanding of newly arrived and unlicensed pharmacists about Canadian pharmacy practice.
Organization Young Women's Christian Association (YWCA) Metro Vancouver
Location of Organization Vancouver, BC
ESDC Contribution $7,781,161
Start Date 2024-01-08
Project Description To improve the labour market integration of internationally educated healthcare professionals, especially skilled refugee women, by providing employment supports in British Columbia and Ontario.
Location of Organization Mississauga, ON
ESDC Contribution $7,550,154
Start Date 2024-01-02
Project Description To help internationally educated healthcare trained health professionals transition into temporary job placements and employment in related healthcare professions, support their accreditation process, and study pathways and obstacles to credential recognition and relocation in both provinces, including rural and remote areas.
Organization Welland Heritage Council and Multicultural Centre
Location of Organization Welland, ON
ESDC Contribution $562,675
Start Date 2024-01-02
Project Description To provide pre-arrival support services to internationally educated health professionals in Ontario through the creation of an informational hub and reducing credential recognition barriers.
Organization Bow Valley College
Location of Organization Calgary, AB
ESDC Contribution $9,493,490
Start Date 2024-01-02
Project Description To pilot a project to expediate the credentialing process for internationally educated registered nurses and licensed practical nurses by bridging gaps between pre-arrival and entry, offering credential recognition support, employer engagement, and tailored services, with plans to expand to three other provinces.
Organization Canadian Association of Medical Radiation Technologists (CAMRT)
Location of Organization Ottawa, ON
ESDC Contribution $2,737,773
Start Date 2024-01-01
Project Description To expedite and streamline the credential assessment of internationally educated medical radiation technologists by simplifying the application process, increasing assessment capacity, and implementing a national bridging program to boost certification and workforce entry.
Organization College of Dental Technologists of Ontario
Location of Organization Scarborough, ON
ESDC Contribution $686,747
Start Date 2023-12-18
Project Description To create a credential pathway for dental laboratory associates/assistants to become registered dental technologists and improve their labour mobility across seven Canadian provinces.
Organization Government of Nova Scotia
Location of Organization Halifax, NS
ESDC Contribution $10,000,000
Start Date 2023-12-04
Project Description To expand Nova Scotia's NICHE Program to address needs in five healthcare professions and provide work experience to reduce foreign credential recognition barriers for internationally trained health professionals.
Organization Progress Career Planning Institute
Location of Organization Toronto, ON
ESDC Contribution $1,514,678
Start Date 2023-12-01
Project Description To support internationally educated healthcare professionals by expediting foreign credential recognition and providing an 11 to 14-week customized program for Registered Nurses and Registered Practitioner Nurses to help them integrate into the Canadian labour market.
Organization Progressive Intercultural Community Services Society (PICS)
Location of Organization Surrey, BC
ESDC Contribution $4,837,419
Start Date 2023-11-20
Project Description To help internationally educated healthcare professionals find and keep healthcare jobs by offering credential evaluations, Canadian work experience, wage subsidies, financial literacy, and personalized support through a Resource Hub.
Organization Supporting Employment and Economic Development (SEED) Winnipeg Inc.
Location of Organization Winnipeg, MB
ESDC Contribution $2,943,603

Project Description To provide loans and support services to eligible internationally educated professionals.

Project Description To provide loans and support services to eligible internationally educated professionals.
